questions.question: Match each LSTM gate to its function.
categories: Decides what to discard from the cell state
Decides what new information to store in the cell state
Decides what to output from the cell state

correct_mapping.Forget gate: Decides what to discard from the cell state
correct_mapping.Input gate: Decides what new information to store in the cell state
correct_mapping.Output gate: Decides what to output from the cell state

questions.explanation: The three LSTM gates each control a different aspect of the cell state. The forget gate removes irrelevant information, the input gate adds new relevant information, and the output gate determines which parts of the cell state become visible as the hidden state.

questions.question: Match each RNN architecture type to an example application.
categories: Music generation from a seed note

correct_mapping.One-to-many: Music generation from a seed note

questions.explanation: Sentiment analysis reads a full review and outputs one label (many-to-one). Music generation takes a starting note and produces a sequence (one-to-many). POS tagging assigns one tag per word (same-length many-to-many). Translation produces a different-length output in another language (different-length many-to-many via encoder-decoder).

questions.question: Match each GRU gate to its purpose.
categories: Controls how much of the past hidden state to forget when computing the candidate
Balances how much of the old hidden state to keep vs. replace with new information

correct_mapping.Reset gate: Controls how much of the past hidden state to forget when computing the candidate
correct_mapping.Update gate: Balances how much of the old hidden state to keep vs. replace with new information

questions.explanation: The reset gate determines how much previous context to use when forming a new candidate hidden state. The update gate then decides the final mix between keeping the old hidden state and adopting the new candidate. Together they provide similar functionality to the LSTM's three gates.

questions.question: Match each RNN problem to its solution.
questions.explanation: LSTMs and GRUs address vanishing gradients through their gating mechanisms and additive cell state updates. Gradient clipping caps the gradient magnitude to prevent parameter explosions. Scheduled sampling gradually transitions from teacher forcing to the model's own predictions during training, reducing the mismatch with inference.

questions.question: Match each Seq2Seq component to its role.
categories: Fixed-length summary passed from encoder to decoder

correct_mapping.Context vector: Fixed-length summary passed from encoder to decoder

questions.explanation: The encoder reads the entire input and compresses it into a context vector (its final hidden state). The decoder is initialized with this context vector and generates the output one token at a time. The context vector is the bridge between the two, carrying all the information about the input.

questions.question: Match each recurrent architecture to its number of gates.
questions.explanation: Vanilla RNNs have no gating mechanism, relying on a simple hidden state update. GRUs introduce two gates to control information flow. LSTMs use three gates plus a separate cell state for the most fine-grained control over memory.

questions.question: Match each concept to whether it describes the hidden state or the cell state in an LSTM.
items: Updated additively by forget and input gates
Directly used as the LSTM's output
questions.explanation: The cell state is the LSTM's long-term memory, updated additively through the forget gate (what to remove) and input gate (what to add). The hidden state is the LSTM's output at each time step, produced by applying the output gate to the cell state. The cell state preserves information; the hidden state exposes selected information.

questions.question: Match each RNN application to its description.
categories: Producing new text one character or word at a time
Converting a sentence in one language to another
Classifying whether a review is positive or negative

correct_mapping.Text generation: Producing new text one character or word at a time
correct_mapping.Machine translation: Converting a sentence in one language to another
correct_mapping.Sentiment analysis: Classifying whether a review is positive or negative

questions.explanation: These are all common RNN applications. Text generation is one-to-many or many-to-many. Machine translation and speech recognition are many-to-many with different-length inputs and outputs. Sentiment analysis is many-to-one, producing a single label from an input sequence.

questions.question: Match each training concept to its description.
categories: Feed ground-truth tokens to the decoder during training
Unroll the RNN and backpropagate through all time steps
Cap gradient magnitude to prevent instability
Gradually shift from ground-truth to model predictions during training

correct_mapping.Teacher forcing: Feed ground-truth tokens to the decoder during training
correct_mapping.BPTT: Unroll the RNN and backpropagate through all time steps
correct_mapping.Gradient clipping: Cap gradient magnitude to prevent instability
correct_mapping.Scheduled sampling: Gradually shift from ground-truth to model predictions during training

questions.explanation: Teacher forcing speeds up decoder training by providing correct inputs. BPTT is the standard method for computing gradients in RNNs. Gradient clipping prevents exploding gradients by capping the gradient norm. Scheduled sampling bridges the gap between teacher forcing during training and self-generated predictions during inference.

questions.question: Match each feature to the architecture it describes: GRU or LSTM.
items: Merges cell state and hidden state
questions.explanation: LSTMs maintain a separate cell state for long-term memory and use three gates (forget, input, output) to manage it. GRUs simplify this by merging the cell state into the hidden state and using only two gates (reset and update). Despite the simpler design, GRUs often achieve comparable performance to LSTMs.
